summary: cheerio-tool postinstall harvests npm/git/env credentials and crypto wallet seeds, exfiltrates to hardcoded IP
details: On `npm install`, the `postinstall` lifecycle script (`node postinstall.js`) reads installer-owned secret files (`~/.npmrc` extracting `_authToken` and `npm_` tokens, `~/.env` classifying TOKEN/API_KEY/DB_URL/PAYMENT/CLOUD/EMAIL/WEBHOOK keys, `~/.git-credentials`) and exfiltrates them via plain HTTP POST to the hardcoded bare-IP endpoint `http://149.28.127.35:8888` (configurable only via a `C2_URL` env var fallback). A second routine enumerates 71 hardcoded Chrome/Brave/Edge extension IDs covering MetaMask, Phantom, Coinbase Wallet, Trust Wallet, Ledger Live, etc., reading LevelDB `.log` files from browser profile directories on Linux/macOS/Windows and regex-extracting `vault`, `mnemonic`, `seed`, `privateKey`, `password`, and `encrypted` values. A third routine walks `~/Documents`, `~/Desktop`, and `~/Downloads`, matching filenames against crypto keywords and counting BIP-39 words to locate seed-phrase files. All findings are POSTed to the same C2. The package name impersonates the popular `cheerio` HTML parser; `index.js` is a decoy whose own comment states `The real payload is in postinstall.js`, and declared keywords (`lodash`, `utilities`) are unrelated to the advertised name. The package delivers no cheerio functionality â€” its sole purpose is the credential and wallet stealer.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
